name: MILES showed metformin shifted metabolic and transcriptomic biomarkers in older adults
summary: MILES is the main direct human biomarker anchor for the AMPK and metabolic modulators baseline.
statement: In the MILES randomized crossover trial, six weeks of metformin in approximately 70-year-old adults lowered 2-hour glucose, insulin AUC, and insulin secretion versus placebo and changed 647 skeletal-muscle genes and 146 adipose genes across metabolic and nonmetabolic pathways linked to aging biology.
population_or_model: Older adults with impaired glucose tolerance
time_horizon: Six weeks each of metformin and placebo
quantitative_note: The source reports 647 differentially expressed muscle genes and 146 differentially expressed adipose genes at FDR <0.05.
caveats: The trial analyzed 14 participants, so precision and generalizability are limited.
The outcomes were metabolic and transcriptomic biomarkers, not physical function, frailty, multimorbidity, or mortality.
The population had impaired glucose tolerance, so results may not apply to insulin-sensitive older adults.
